Module me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.core
Package me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.backend.window

Interface FlixelWindow

All Superinterfaces:
FlixelShakeable
All Known Implementing Classes:
FlixelNoopWindow
public interface FlixelWindow extends FlixelShakeable
Desktop window controls that stay safe on every platform.

Use Flixel.window after Flixel.initialize(me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.FlixelGame). The implementation only adjusts backdrop drawing and, on desktop with a transparent-capable framebuffer, an end-of-frame alpha fix so normal gameplay is not composited through the desktop unless this mode is on.

Desktop games default to an alpha-capable framebuffer (see FlixelGame.isTransparentFramebufferRequested()). Call setDesktopTransparencyActive(boolean) any time to show the real desktop through unused pixels, or turn it back off.

Example: 


 Flixel.window.setDesktopTransparencyActive(true);  // Sprites over the desktop.
 // ...
 Flixel.window.setDesktopTransparencyActive(false); // Normal opaque letterboxing again.

This interface extends FlixelShakeable so you can pass Flixel.window to FlixelTween.shake(me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.functional.FlixelShakeable, me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.util.FlixelAxes, float, me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.tween.settings.FlixelTweenSettings) when you want the OS window itself to jitter.

See Also:
  • Flixel.window

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    default void
    applyTransparentWorldBackdrop()
    Same as setDesktopTransparencyActive(true).
    default void

    Moves the window horizontally by a delta in screen pixels.

    changeY(int deltaY)
    Moves the window vertically by a delta in screen pixels.

    Same as setDesktopTransparencyActive(false).

    Height used to scale FlixelShakeUnit.FRACTION on the Y axis.

    setAbsorbCloseRequests(boolean absorb)
    When true, the GLFW close event is absorbed so the window does not exit until you stop absorbing or call Gdx.app.exit() yourself.

    Turns desktop-composited transparency on or off.

    Sets the shake channel to absolute coordinates (typically base plus jitter).

    Sets whether the window uses native title bar and border decorations, when supported.

    When true, requests a floating (often "always on top") window using GLFW_FLOATING where the backend supports it.

  • Method Details

    • getShakeX

      default float getShakeX()
      Current shake channel X (for example graphic offset or world X).
      Specified by:
      getShakeX in interface FlixelShakeable
      Returns:
      Shake X before jitter is applied.

    • getShakeY

      default float getShakeY()
      Current shake channel Y.
      Specified by:
      getShakeY in interface FlixelShakeable
      Returns:
      Shake Y before jitter is applied.

    • setShake

      default void setShake(float x, float y)
      Sets the shake channel to absolute coordinates (typically base plus jitter).
      Specified by:
      setShake in interface FlixelShakeable
      Parameters:
      x - New shake X.
      y - New shake Y.

    • getShakeWidth

      default float getShakeWidth()
      Width used to scale FlixelShakeUnit.FRACTION on the X axis. Values 0 or less mean the tween treats intensity like plain pixels on that axis.
      Specified by:
      getShakeWidth in interface FlixelShakeable
      Returns:
      Reference width for fractional shake, or non-positive to fall back.

    • getShakeHeight

      default float getShakeHeight()
      Height used to scale FlixelShakeUnit.FRACTION on the Y axis. Values 0 or less mean the tween treats intensity like plain pixels on that axis.
      Specified by:
      getShakeHeight in interface FlixelShakeable
      Returns:
      Reference height for fractional shake, or non-positive to fall back.

    • isTransparentFramebufferRequested

      default boolean isTransparentFramebufferRequested()
      When true, the LWJGL3 launcher enables Lwjgl3ApplicationConfiguration#setTransparentFramebuffer(boolean). This is true by default so runtime transparency can work; set false before launch only if you need a classic opaque framebuffer or hit a driver issue (then setDesktopTransparencyActive(boolean) cannot composite with the desktop).
      Returns:
      current launch-time request for an alpha-capable default framebuffer.

    • setDesktopTransparencyActive

      default void setDesktopTransparencyActive(boolean active)
      Turns desktop-composited transparency on or off. When true, clears and camera backdrop fills use alpha zero so unchanged pixels show whatever is behind the window (when the framebuffer was created with transparency support). When false, restores backdrop colors cached the first time transparency was enabled this session, or falls back to opaque Color.BLACK if transparency was never enabled.
      Parameters:
      active - true to composite with the desktop through alpha; false for a normal opaque window interior.

    • isDesktopTransparencyActive

      default boolean isDesktopTransparencyActive()
      Returns:
      last value applied by setDesktopTransparencyActive(boolean) for this game session.

    • applyTransparentWorldBackdrop

      default void applyTransparentWorldBackdrop()
      Same as setDesktopTransparencyActive(true).

    • clearDesktopTransparency

      default void clearDesktopTransparency()
      Same as setDesktopTransparencyActive(false).

    • getWindowOpacity

      default float getWindowOpacity()
      Returns:
      The current opacity level of the game's window.

    • setWindowOpacity

      default void setWindowOpacity(float opacity)
      Sets whole-window opacity where the backend supports it (GLFW 3.4+, LWJGL3 desktop).
      Parameters:
      opacity - Opacity in [0, 1]; non-finite values are ignored.

    • supportsWindowOpacity

      boolean supportsWindowOpacity()
      Returns:
      true if setWindowOpacity(float) can affect the window on this session.

    • setWindowDecorated

      default void setWindowDecorated(boolean decorated)
      Sets whether the window uses native title bar and border decorations, when supported.
      Parameters:
      decorated - false for a borderless window.

    • supportsSetWindowDecorated

      default boolean supportsSetWindowDecorated()
      Returns:
      true if setWindowDecorated(boolean) is supported on this session.

    • getX

      default int getX()
      Window X position in screen coordinates, when supported.
      Returns:
      horizontal position, or 0 when unknown.

    • getY

      default int getY()
      Window Y position in screen coordinates, when supported.
      Returns:
      vertical position, or 0 when unknown.

    • setX

      default void setX(int x)
      Sets the window's X position in screen coordinates, when supported.
      Parameters:
      x - Target X in screen coordinates.

    • setY

      default void setY(int y)
      Sets the window's Y position in screen coordinates, when supported.
      Parameters:
      y - Target Y in screen coordinates.

    • setPosition

      default void setPosition(int x, int y)
      Moves the window so its client area origin is placed at the given screen coordinates, when supported.
      Parameters:
      x - Target X in screen coordinates.
      y - Target Y in screen coordinates.

    • changeX

      default void changeX(int deltaX)
      Moves the window horizontally by a delta in screen pixels.
      Parameters:
      deltaX - Pixels to add to the current X position (negative values move left).

    • changeY

      default void changeY(int deltaY)
      Moves the window vertically by a delta in screen pixels.
      Parameters:
      deltaY - Pixels to add to the current Y position (negative values move up on backends that use upper-left origin).

    • bringWindowToForeground

      default void bringWindowToForeground()
      Asks the OS to focus this game window (desktop only where GLFW allows it).

      CAUTION: Pulling focus away from another application is disruptive. It's advised you warn players ahead of time on your store page, in a first-run dialog, or in an in-game settings label before calling this from normal gameplay.

    • supportsBringWindowToForeground

      default boolean supportsBringWindowToForeground()
      Returns:
      true if bringWindowToForeground() can run on this session.

    • setWindowFloating

      default void setWindowFloating(boolean floating)
      When true, requests a floating (often "always on top") window using GLFW_FLOATING where the backend supports it.

      CAUTION: Keeping the game above everything else can hide important system UI or other apps. Disclose this in plain language before you enable it, and ideally expose a user-visible toggle.

      Parameters:
      floating - true to keep the window above normal stacking, false for default stacking.

    • supportsSetWindowFloating

      default boolean supportsSetWindowFloating()
      Returns:
      true if setWindowFloating(boolean) may take effect on this session.

    • setAbsorbCloseRequests

      default void setAbsorbCloseRequests(boolean absorb)
      When true, the GLFW close event is absorbed so the window does not exit until you stop absorbing or call Gdx.app.exit() yourself.

      CAUTION: Players expect the window close control to quit. If you absorb close requests, you must explain that ahead of time (splash text, settings, store description) and always provide another obvious way to exit.

      Parameters:
      absorb - true to cancel the default close handling from the windowing system.

    • supportsAbsorbCloseRequests

      default boolean supportsAbsorbCloseRequests()
      Returns:
      true if setAbsorbCloseRequests(boolean) is wired for this session.

    • isAbsorbCloseRequestsEnabled

      default boolean isAbsorbCloseRequestsEnabled()
      Returns:
      true while close absorption is enabled and the listener chain is active. When supportsAbsorbCloseRequests() is false, this is always false.

    • isWindowFloating

      default boolean isWindowFloating()
      Returns:
      true when the window is currently floating (always on top), if the backend can query it. When unsupported, returns false.

    • isWindowDecorated

      default boolean isWindowDecorated()
      Returns:
      true when the window has native decorations (title bar and border), if the backend can query it. When unsupported, returns true so game code treats the common case as decorated.
